elements.name: Hydrogen
elements.description: Hydrogen is the lightest element and a common building block in water, acids, and many organic compounds.

elements.name: Oxygen
elements.description: Oxygen supports combustion and appears in water, oxides, and many molecules found in air and living systems.

elements.name: Sodium
elements.description: Sodium is a soft reactive metal that often forms salts and basic compounds.

elements.name: Chlorine
elements.description: Chlorine is a reactive halogen used in salts, disinfectants, and many industrial chemicals.

elements.name: Carbon
elements.description: Carbon is the backbone of organic chemistry and can form chains, rings, and many stable compounds.

elements.name: Nitrogen
elements.description: Nitrogen makes up most of Earth's atmosphere and appears in ammonia, proteins, and fertilizers.

elements.name: Sulfur
elements.description: Sulfur is a yellow nonmetal often found in acids, minerals, and volcanic gases.

elements.name: Potassium
elements.description: Potassium is a highly reactive alkali metal important for salts and many biological processes.

elements.name: Calcium
elements.description: Calcium is a common metal in minerals and is essential for bones, shells, and cement chemistry.

elements.name: Magnesium
elements.description: Magnesium is a light metal used in alloys and forms bright-burning oxides.

elements.name: Phosphorus
elements.description: Phosphorus is used in fertilizers, matches, and energy-carrying molecules in living cells.

elements.name: Iron
elements.description: Iron is a strong transition metal used in steel and forms several important oxides.

compounds.name: Hydrogen
compounds.description: Hydrogen gas is the lightest molecule and an important fuel and industrial reagent.

compounds.name: Oxygen
compounds.description: Oxygen gas is essential for breathing and helps many reactions burn or release energy.

compounds.name: Chlorine
compounds.description: Chlorine gas is a reactive halogen used in disinfectants and chemical manufacturing.

compounds.name: Water
compounds.description: Water is one of the most important compounds for life and covers most of Earth's surface.

compounds.name: Hydrogen Peroxide
compounds.description: Hydrogen peroxide is a reactive liquid often used as a disinfectant and bleaching agent.

compounds.name: Hydrochloric Acid
compounds.description: Hydrochloric acid is a strong acid found in stomach acid and used in many industrial processes.

compounds.name: Salt
compounds.description: Sodium chloride is table salt, a familiar mineral important in food and chemistry.

compounds.name: Sodium Hydroxide
compounds.description: Sodium hydroxide is a strong base widely used in soap making, cleaning, and industry.

compounds.name: Sodium Oxide
compounds.description: Sodium oxide is a basic oxide formed from sodium and oxygen.

compounds.name: Sodium Peroxide
compounds.description: Sodium peroxide is a reactive oxygen-rich compound that can release oxygen in chemical reactions.

compounds.name: Carbon Monoxide
compounds.description: Carbon monoxide is a toxic gas produced by incomplete combustion.

compounds.name: Carbon Dioxide
compounds.description: Carbon dioxide is a common gas in air and a major product of respiration and combustion.

compounds.name: Methane
compounds.description: Methane is the main component of natural gas and a simple fuel molecule.

compounds.name: Carbonic Acid
compounds.description: Carbonic acid forms when carbon dioxide dissolves in water and helps make drinks fizzy.

compounds.name: Ammonia
compounds.description: Ammonia is a pungent gas used in fertilizers, cleaning products, and industrial chemistry.

compounds.name: Nitric Oxide
compounds.description: Nitric oxide is a reactive gas with roles in chemistry and biology.

compounds.name: Nitrogen Dioxide
compounds.description: Nitrogen dioxide is a brown reactive gas often linked to air pollution.

compounds.name: Sulfur Dioxide
compounds.description: Sulfur dioxide is a sharp-smelling gas produced in volcanic activity and fuel burning.

compounds.name: Sulfur Trioxide
compounds.description: Sulfur trioxide is a highly reactive sulfur oxide important in acid production.

compounds.name: Potassium Chloride
compounds.description: Potassium chloride is a common salt used in fertilizers and chemistry.

compounds.name: Calcium Oxide
compounds.description: Calcium oxide, also called quicklime, is important in cement and industrial processes.

compounds.name: Magnesium Oxide
compounds.description: Magnesium oxide is a stable oxide used in refractory materials and chemistry.

compounds.name: Phosphine
compounds.description: Phosphine is a toxic phosphorus-containing gas used in specialized chemistry.

compounds.name: Iron(III) Oxide
compounds.description: Iron(III) oxide is a common iron oxide found in rust and mineral pigments.

themes.name: Basic
themes.description: Start with the most familiar compounds and learn the core drag-to-connect rules.

themes.name: Gases
themes.description: Build simple gases and get comfortable with small two-atom and three-atom molecules.

themes.name: Acids & Bases
themes.description: Focus on reactive compounds that introduce acidic and basic chemistry.

themes.name: Oxides
themes.description: Practice oxygen-rich compounds where structure starts to matter more.
